MODULE ONE – Positioning
2
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Executive Summary:
• 	Positioning is how the market sees you. What do you look like from their perspective?
• 	Here’s an example: Toyota and Lexus are the same company. Toyota appeals to the
everyday consumer/driver, but they decided they wanted to create a new luxury
brand. Instead of repositioning their existing brand, they created a new premium
luxury brand - Lexus.
• 	Your market is the same - people will always want to position you in the low, middle,
or high end. You need and want to be seen in the high-end of your market as a leader
that provides massive value.
• 	A strongly executed product launch instantly positions you as a guru and expert, that’s
why PLF is so powerful!
• 	Benefits of strong positioning
o 	Deal flow: opportunities to partner with other businesses
o 	Speaking engagements
o 	Joint Venture deals
o 	Charging high prices for your products and having higher margins
• 	Everything you do in your business either builds or degrades your business. That’s
why it is so key to be aware of all your actions.
• 	Ultimately, your positioning builds more positioning, and this allows you to go from
launching a single product to building a valuable business.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

TRANSCRIPT
MODULE ONE – Positioning
3
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
TRANSCRIPT:
Our next strategy is positioning. That is basically how the market
sees you, what they see as your value or prestige.
Think of Chevy versus BMW in the automobile market. Chevy here in the
United States is sort of the “every person’s car,” just not super budget, but not fancy
whatsoever. Then you have BMW, which they’re not even cars, they’re “the ultimate
driving machine,” so BMW is pretty high end. Then there’s above BMW, you could go
to Bentley or Rolls Royce. These are different layers of positioning.
Think about Toyota and Lexus. Same car company really. Toyota wanted to
create a premium brand, they wanted to start creating premium cars, but they
already had this positioning sort of like Chevy with the every person’s car.
So rather than trying to recreate their positioning and convince people that
now Toyotas are super high end, they created a new company and called it Lexus.
That’s how they got that positioning, they created this new car and started making
really high end cars called Lexus.
It’s a lot like that for anyone in any market and any kind of business. The
people in your market want to sort out where are you – are you high end, mid-tier, or
low end – where do you fit in?
In general, in this market I think you want to be seen as a leader who provides
massive value. That’s the positioning I want and I think that’s the positioning that I
have. I think it’s a great positioning for you to pursue as well. Be seen as a leader, be
seen as someone who over delivers and provides massive value.
You don’t want to compete on price. Generally, in this market that’s a bad
thing to do. You want to compete on value. So you’re never going to see me going up
and trying to be the lowest priced course in any area. I want to be the highest priced
or among the highest priced, because I want to provide a massive value.
When you do that, high end positioning is a great thing because it allows you
to charge higher prices, and higher prices – well, I’ll get to that in just a second. The
high end there’s so much goodness.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

The thing to remember is a well-run Product Launch, a well-orchestrated
Product Launch, absolutely maximizes your positioning in the market.
It raises your stature in the market. And it does it pretty much instantly and
almost automatically, especially if you’re doing a JV Launch.
If you do a Joint Venture Launch, because now you might have five or 10 joint
venture partners and they’re mailing their lists and they’re talking about how
awesome you are. That’s the way to get massive guru positioning right away, massive
expert positioning almost immediately.
When I came out with Product Launch Formula, I did that first workshop and
sold six of them. Then I came out with a JV Launch and I was able to get strong JV
support because a lot of the top JV’s in the market I had helped them out with
launches. I had taught them how to do launches, so they were happy to come back
and promote for me.
So when I came into the market I had been into selling stuff online for eight
or nine years, so I was very experienced, but in the market of teaching people about
internet marketing no one knew who I was.
I came out with a JV Launch and the top 20 joint venture partners, the top 20
leaders in the market started mailing their list saying, “Jeff is awesome. Jeff is really
brilliant. Go check out Jeff’s stuff.” I’m instantly seen at the top end of the market as
an expert, as a guru. That’s great positioning.
Some of the things you get from that kind of positioning is you get
deal flow.
Deal flow is awesome. By that I just mean all kinds of deals start
coming to you. These days I get so many deals, so many people coming to me and
they want me to promote a product, they want to promote my product, they want me
to be part of their product, they want to give me their product and have me promote
their product as mine, they want to give me a percentage of the business, they want
to give me points for this, they want me in an advisory role. It’s great to have that
kind of deal flow.
With positioning you get deal flow. You get great speaking gigs. You get great
joint venture deals. You get better deals on those joint ventures than anyone else if
you’re at the top of the market. All the best in the market comes to you when you

have strong positioning. That’s a really key thing. Strong positioning gives you all the
good juju in the market.
Generally, the people with the best positioning can charge the highest prices.
This is the key to Product Launch Formula. You can almost always be
charging at the high end of the market just by doing a launch, because it’s such a
great sales process and it gives you such great positioning. Charging higher prices
makes it a lot easier to grow your business, because it gives you higher margins and
those margins mean more opportunity.
That means if you can go out and afford to pay your affiliates $XX per sale, or
everyone else is paying $X per sale but you can pay two times that because you have
that positioning, because you have the higher prices, then you absolutely win.
This is the important piece - really important.
Everything you do in your business either builds your
positioning or it degrades it.
Product Launches are great for maximizing your positioning, because you
build that authority, you build the reciprocity, you build the likability, you build the
social proof.
A few things to remember....
Positioning builds positioning. It’s sort of like being a celebrity in
Hollywood. If you do really great and you have this hit movie then more good parts
are going to come to you and you’re going to get paid more for those parts. Then
you’re getting these great parts, so since you’re getting great parts you’re creating
great movies, and that builds your positioning more and more.
It’s like deal flow. You’re this actor and you do great in a movie, all of a
sudden these great parts come to you, so you can be in more great movies and you’re
playing these great parts so your positioning keeps on growing. Positioning builds

positioning.
Now, what you have to watch for is, like I said, everything either builds your
positioning or degrades it. Going back to that celebrity, you might be on top of the
world having all these great parts, well you don’t want to be the drunken celebrity
running down Hollywood Boulevard in your underwear and destroy your positioning
overnight.
This whole entire chunk on strategy where I was talking about don’t give away
your product three months after you sell it for a high price, don’t do silly things like
that. Take care of your partners, take care of your clients, even take care of your
prospects, because positioning builds positioning and you can destroy your
positioning pretty quickly by being that drunken celebrity.
I’m going to be covering positioning throughout this course. We’re going to be
going in depth, I’m going to be showing you examples that build it, that degrade it,
and how to build your launches. The thing to remember is if you follow the plan, if
you follow Product Launch Formula, then this is all going to happen for you
automatically. You’re automatically going to get that premium BMW positioning just
by going through this process.
